video:video.video:title: Singer D4vd is arrested on suspicion of killing of a 14-year-old girl found dead in his Tesla
video:video.video:description: Singer D4vd has been arrested on suspicion of killing a 14-year-old girl whose decomposed body was found last year in his apparently abandoned Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills, authorities said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Colombia's environment minister says Middle East crisis should speed energy transition
video:video.video:description: Ahead of talks among some 50 countries about moving away from polluting fuels like oil and gas, Colombia's environment minister is arguing that the war in Iran underscores the need for a fast transition to clean energies like solar, wind, and geothermal.

video:video.video:title: AP finds ICE officers working before background checks, despite prior issues
video:video.video:description: Some new ICE officers began work before passing background checks and had past problems. In January, ICE said it added 12,000 officers to double its force for Trump’s deportation campaign.

video:video.video:title: Mounted New York police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in Manhattan
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Sparse shelves amid liquor shortage in Mississippi
video:video.video:description: A backlog at Mississippi's alcohol distribution center is causing shortages throughout the state. (AP Video: Sophie Bates)

video:video.video:title: Descendants say the theft of body parts from Pennsylvania grave sites is not a victimless crime
video:video.video:description: Descendants whose relatives’ burial sites were disturbed in a crime spree involving the theft of more than 100 bodies from Pennsylvania cemeteries speak outside the court after the defendant waived his right to an evidentiary hearing.

video:video.video:title: Pete Hegseth reads a prayer adapted from 'Pulp Fiction' during Pentagon service
video:video.video:description: Hegseth recited what the lead planner had delivered to their team ahead of the mission. It was a customized prayer they call CSAR 25:17.

video:video.video:title: You aren't the only one who just sits in the car before or after a long day
video:video.video:description: Pausing to decompress in a parked car can help you reset, if you keep a few things in mind. That temporary pause can act as a buffer between one part of the day and the next.

video:video.video:title: What you need to know as US and Iran say Strait of Hormuz fully open to commercial vessels
video:video.video:description: Iran said Friday it fully reopened the Strait of Hormuz to commercial vessels, but President Donald Trump said the American blockade on Iranian ships and ports “will remain in full force” until Tehran reaches a deal with the U.S., including on its nuclear program.

video:video.video:title: Why wildfires are burning longer than they used to
video:video.video:description: Wildfires used to die down and even stop at night with cooler temperatures and lower humidity. But a study says climate change is making burning weather more around the clock in North America because night is becoming warmer and drier.

video:video.video:title: How Elizabeth Banks pulled off acting tiny for new series
video:video.video:description: In Peacock's "The Miniature Wife," Elizabeth Banks plays a woman shrunk by her husband, played by Matthew Macfadyen. (April 17)

video:video.video:title: Matthew Macfadyen created the illusion of having a mini-sized wife in new series
video:video.video:description: In Peacock's "The Miniature Wife," Macfadyen plays a man who shrinks his wife, played by Elizabeth Banks. (April 17)

video:video.video:title: Israelis and displaced Lebanese react to ceasefire
video:video.video:description: A fragile calm settled over parts of Lebanon on Friday as a 10-day ceasefire brokered by the United States took hold between Israel and Hezbollah, prompting thousands of displaced families to begin the journey home — even as uncertainty, destruction and Israeli warnings against going back to parts o

video:video.video:title: Mounted police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in New York
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Transgender girl leans into track season and not upcoming Supreme Court decision
video:video.video:description: Becky Pepper-Jackson is part of a potentially history-making case before the U.S. Supreme Court this summer.

video:video.video:title: House extends surveillance powers until April 30 after late-night revolt sinks GOP plan
video:video.video:description: The House has approved a short-term renewal until April 30 of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 17
video:video.video:description: AP Top Stories April 17

video:video.video:title: Displaced relieved at Lebanon ceasefire but shocked by extent of destruction as they return home
video:video.video:description: A 10-day truce appeared to be holding in Lebanon early Friday, promising a pause in fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and possibly clearing one major obstacle to a deal between Iran and the United States and Israel to end weeks of devastating war.

video:video.video:title: Hands-on with Artemis II crew's luxury space watch
video:video.video:description: A watch built for space is back in the spotlight in Geneva. Omega’s Speedmaster line has been worn by astronauts for decades and is again drawing attention after being seen on the wrists of the crew of Artemis II.

video:video.video:title: Overflowing Wisconsin River carries sturgeon into parking area
video:video.video:description: Video from Green Bay TV station WBAY shows sturgeon swimming in a flooded parking lot in Outagamie County, in central Wisconsin after torrential rainstorms drenched the state.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran war 'going swimmingly' despite rising costs, casualties and uncertainty
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he was feeling positive about the Iran war despite energy price spikes, death and destruction, and anxiety over NATO and the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Indonesian rescuers recover bodies from site of helicopter crash on Borneo which killed eight
video:video.video:description: A helicopter flying between palm oil plantations on Indonesia's Borneo Island has crashed, killing the eight people on board.

video:video.video:title: Iranian Jews commemorate slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Members of Iran's Jewish community commemorated Iran's late supreme leader on Thursday. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in an airstrike on his residence on Feb. 28, the day the United States and Israel started a war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Solar farm helps Kentucky sheep farmer profit, grow flock
video:video.video:description: Kentucky sheep farmer Daniel Bell has been expanding thanks to a partnership with a solar company that pays him to graze his flock among the panels. (AP Video: Joshua A.

video:video.video:title: EU officials in Hungary to discuss unlocking billions of euros held while Orbán was in charge
video:video.video:description: European Union officials were in Budapest, Hungary on Friday to meet with the team of Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar to discuss the unlocking of funds frozen by the bloc during the time of outgoing premier Viktor Orbán.

video:video.video:title: Mourners gather for funeral of eight-year-old boy killed in Russia strike
video:video.video:description: Bohdan Serhiiev, was killed in a Russian drone strike on the central Ukrainian city of Cherkasy earlier in the week.

video:video.video:title: Hunt on for robbers who held 25 people hostage at bank in Italy
video:video.video:description: Robbers broke into a bank in Naples on Thursday, holding 25 people hostage before escaping through the sewers, having drilled a hole in the floor during the night, according to the Italian news agency LaPresse.

video:video.video:title: Original Winnie-the-Pooh sketches for sale
video:video.video:description: London's Peter Harrington store is hosting an exhibition of never-before-seen Winnie-the-Pooh drawings until April 27, before they go on sale.

video:video.video:title: Pope urges young people to resist temptation of corruption as he celebrates mass in Cameroon
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV on Friday urged Cameroon’s young people to resist the temptation of corruption and instead work to serve the common good as he celebrated a mass before thousands of people in one of the largest expected turnouts of his 11-day, four-nation Africa journey.

video:video.video:title: Haitians say they are suffering as soaring oil prices deepen food crisis
video:video.video:description: A displaced woman in Haiti describes how extreme poverty and rising oil prices have forced her family to survive on just one meal a day amid a worsening food crisis.

video:video.video:title: ICE acting director Todd Lyons will resign at the end of May
video:video.video:description: Under Lyons' leadership, the agency got a massive infusion of cash through Congress, which it used to expand hiring and detention capabilities, and it ramped up arrests.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: A fragile calm settled over parts of Lebanon on Friday as a 10-day ceasefire brokered by the United States took hold between Israel and Hezbollah, prompting thousands of displaced families to begin the journey home — even as uncertainty, destruction and Israeli warnings against going back to parts o

video:video.video:title: Trump promotes his tax cuts in Las Vegas as he seeks voters ahead of midterm elections
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is betting that the tax cuts he signed into law last year will resonate with voters in Las Vegas, as he pushes a focus on economic issues for this year’s midterm elections.

video:video.video:title: Stories of Black and Indigenous patriots come into focus as nation remembers American Revolution
video:video.video:description: The role of Black and Indigenous people in the American Revolution is getting greater attention as the country remembers their contribution to America's independence.

video:video.video:title: This must be the place: David Byrne backstage at Coachella | AP interview
video:video.video:description: David Byrne sits down with Associated Press entertainment journalist Liam McEwan backstage at the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival to discuss the surge of younger fans discovering his work, his creative process, and the unpredictability of hits like “Burning Down the House.”

video:video.video:title: Footage shows trees uprooted after a tornado hit a village in Illinois
video:video.video:description: A likely tornado tore through a small village in northwest Illinois on Friday, ripping down power lines and roofs, uprooting trees.

video:video.video:title: UK PM 'absolutely furious' over latest developments in vetting of Mandelson for US role
video:video.video:description: Following the revelation in the Guardian newspaper, the UK government said Prime Minister Keir Starmer was not aware the Foreign Office had overruled a security vetting process for Peter Mandelson to become UK ambassador to Washington “until earlier this week.”

video:video.video:title: Trump’s plan for a 250-foot Triumphal Arch in Washington gets approval
video:video.video:description: The concept for President Donald Trump’s 250-foot Triumphal Arch in Washington has been approved by a federal agency. Here's what to know.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, April 17th: U.S. and Iran say Strait of Hormuz now open to commercial shipping; Thousands return home amid ceasefire in Lebanon; Accused Charlie Kirk shooter seeks ban on cameras in courtroom; Optimism over Iran war sends stocks soaring.

video:video.video:title: $13 train fare spikes to $150 for World Cup fans attending matches in New Jersey
video:video.video:description: New Jersey Transit officials announced Friday that fans trying to get to MetLife Stadium from New York City can expect to shell out $150 for a round-trip train fare for each match.

video:video.video:title: Trump says 'a lot of good things are happening' as talks continue with Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said talks with Iran are progressing, calling it “a big day” and downplaying any significant differences between the two sides.

video:video.video:title: France, UK welcome Hormuz reopening but press for lasting freedom of navigation
video:video.video:description: The leaders of France and the U.K. on Friday welcomed the announcement by Iran and the U.S. that the Strait of Hormuz is open, but said freedom of navigation must be permanently restored to the key oil route choked by the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Charlie Kirk's Turning Point USA expands into high schools
video:video.video:description: Republican leaders across the U.S. are encouraging chapters of the conservative political group Turning Point USA in public high schools in the wake last year’s assassination of co-founder Charlie Kirk, an effort they describe as countering the suppression of conservative voices in education.

video:video.video:title: BINI bring Filipino pride to Coachella
video:video.video:description: Members of the girl group BINI reflect on their “historical” Coachella performance — bringing Filipino pride to the desert stage: "We're carrying this heavy weight of love."

video:video.video:title: Markets spike, oil drops sharply on opening of Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Oil prices dropped back to where they were in the early days of the Iran war, and the S&P 500 hit another record Friday after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is open again for commercial tankers carrying crude from the Persian Gulf to customers worldwide.

video:video.video:title: Former housemates speak about man charged in Atlanta-area killings
video:video.video:description: Two women who once lived in the same house as a man charged in a string of deadly attacks in the Atlanta suburbs speak out about his behavior prior to his arrest (AP Video: Ron Harris)

video:video.video:title: World record set for largest gathering of people in dinosaur costumes
video:video.video:description: The University of Calgary celebrated its 60th anniversary on Saturday, April 11, by breaking the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of people dressed in dinosaur costumes, according to a statement from the school.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US will go into Iran and excavate uranium
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump suggested in a speech in Arizona that Iran has agreed to hand over its enriched uranium.

video:video.video:title: Mounted police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in New York
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Trump touts 'good news' in Iran negotiations, rejects notion of tolls on Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: After declaring the Strait of Hormuz open, Tehran maintained its grip on transit and threatened to close it again if the US kept in place its blockade of Iranian ships and ports.

video:video.video:title: Eyewitnesses recount three deadly Israeli strikes on medics in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: “We went to help. These aren't just civilians, they're our colleagues, so it was a little emotional for us,” Mohammed Jaber, 43, told The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Pope calls for 'structures of solidarity' to help marginalised as he wraps up Cameroon visit
video:video.video:description: Friday marked the half-way point in Leo’s 11-day tour of four African nations. He leaves Saturday for Angola, another country blessed with oil and other natural resources, but where a third of the population lives on less than $2.15 a day.

video:video.video:title: Gaza's garbage crisis adds another threat to the health of Palestinians
video:video.video:description: Since a fragile ceasefire began last October, conflict in the region has calmed, but much of the territory remains in ruins with no clear timeline for reconstruction.

video:video.video:title: Iran reimposes restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, accusing US of violating deal to reopen it
video:video.video:description: The announcement was made the day after U.S. President Donald Trump said the American blockade “will remain in full force” until Tehran reaches a deal with the U.S., including on its nuclear program.

video:video.video:title: Iran says it has closed Strait of Hormuz again over US blockade
video:video.video:description: Confusion over the critical chokepoint threatened to deepen the energy crisis roiling the global economy and push the two countries toward renewed conflict, even as mediators expressed confidence a new deal was within reach.

video:video.video:title: You can put beef tallow and salmon sperm on your face. But should you?
video:video.video:description: Animal-derived skincare is trending, from salmon sperm facials to moisturizers made with beef tallow.

video:video.video:title: Iran's supreme leader vows more defeats for US and Israel at hands of Iranian navy
video:video.video:description: Iran's supreme leader vows more defeats for US and Israel at hands of Iranian navy

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV says he doesn't want to debate Trump, but will keep preaching the Gospel
video:video.video:description: Speaking to reporters, Pope Leo XIV addressed head the spiralling back-and-forth saga of Trump’s critiques of his peace message, which have dominated news headlines this week.

video:video.video:title: Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics, including the controversial drug ibogaine
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Saturday directed his administration to speed up reviews of certain psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, which recently has been embraced by combat veterans and conservative lawmakers despite having serious safety risks.

video:video.video:title: French soldier killed in attack on peacekeepers in Lebanon on second day of ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The attack near the southern Lebanese village of Ghandouriyeh came after a 10-day ceasefire went into effect at midnight Thursday between Israel and Lebanon's militant Hezbollah group.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran 'got a little cute,' but there are good conversations happening
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump says that U.S. talks with Iran are going well and that he expects to have more information “by the end of the day

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian police shoot and kill a gunman who killed at least 6 and took hostages in a supermarket
video:video.video:description: A gunman who killed at least six people in Kyiv, Ukraine's capital, before taking hostages and barricading himself inside a supermarket on Saturday has been shot and killed by police, Ukrainian officials said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, April 18th: Iran fully closes Strait of Hormuz over US blockade; Mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves six dead; Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics; Powerful winds and reported tornadoes rip through the Midwest.

video:video.video:title: Powerful winds and reported tornadoes rip through the Midwest, leaving heavy damage but no deaths
video:video.video:description: A trail of damaged homes and buildings dotted a wide swath of the U.S. on Saturday after a burst of destructive winds and reported tornadoes tore off roofs, uprooted trees and rendered rural roads impassable with debris.

video:video.video:title: 500 fans turn Santiago into a Star Wars galaxy
video:video.video:description: Star Wars fans gathered Saturday for the third edition of the “Parade of the Galaxies,” an event featuring a variety of cosplayers who showcased their best costumes along a main avenue in central Santiago.

video:video.video:title: Reenactors hope to keep the memories of fallen American Revolution soldiers alive in Lexington
video:video.video:description: The Lexington Minutemen are marking the anniversary of the Battle of Lexington in Massachusetts on Saturday, as they do every year.

video:video.video:title: Lights out in Havana’s nightlife after U.S. oil blockade
video:video.video:description: There are few lights on the streets, no families out for a stroll, no couples canoodling in central Havana. (AP video: Ariel Fernández & Milexsy Durán)

video:video.video:title: Chernobyl's radioactive landscape testament to nature’s resilience
video:video.video:description: Four decades on, Chernobyl remains too dangerous for humans. But the wildlife has moved back in. Wolves now prowl the vast no-man’s-land spanning Ukraine and Belarus, and brown bears have returned after more than a century.

video:video.video:title: Obama and Mamdani sing Wheels on the Bus to preschoolers
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama met with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani for the first time on Saturday and led Bronx preschoolers in a singalong. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Belgium’s Beguinages: Tranquil oases in a world of noise and distraction
video:video.video:description: Nestled in a sea of yellow daffodils lies an oasis of calm and tranquility founded in 1245: the Princely Beguinage Ten Wijngaerde of Bruges.
